agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-sale lifecycle. The rules govern inventory reservation, pricing discounts, order immutability, and refund eligibility — all standard bounded contexts in online retail.","bounded_context":"Order Management & Checkout (with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions, and Returns)","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of line items a customer intends to purchase. No inventory is reserved at this stage."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-bound hold on inventory units, created when checkout begins, guaranteeing availability for up to 15 minutes pending payment."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where payment is not confirmed within 15 minutes, causing all associated inventory reservations to be released."},{"term":"Promotion","definition":"A seller-defined pricing adjustment applied to a specific product (e.g., 20% off). A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A buyer-supplied code that applies a pricing reduction at the order level. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The terminal state of a successful checkout. After this point, the order becomes immutable."},{"term":"Order Immutability","definition":"The invariant that an order's contents, pricing, and metadata cannot be modified after payment is confirmed."},{"term":"Listed Product","definition":"A product visible in the catalog. Visibility does not imply purchasability — zero-inventory products may still be listed but cannot be bought."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-calendar-day period after delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Delivery Date","definition":"The date the order is marked as delivered; serves as the anchor for the refund window calculation."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Inventory is NOT reserved when items are added to the cart. Reservation occurs only when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If inventory were reserved at cart-add time, phantom holds would deplete available stock system-wide, corrupting inventory counts. This is a data/state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Adding an item to a cart does not decrement available inventory or create any reservation record."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An inventory reservation must be released if payment is not confirmed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy. A VP could change it to 10 or 20 minutes without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"Given a checkout started at time T, if no payment is confirmed by T+15 minutes, all associated inventory reservations are released and inventory is restored to available."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product can have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on the same product create ambiguous pricing — the system cannot determine which price to display or charge. This is a data consistency constraint that, if violated, produces corrupt pricing state.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to activate a second promotion on a product that already has an active promotion must fail or deactivate the existing promotion first."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive on a single order. Only one discount mechanism may apply.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Stacking discounts without explicit business logic for combination produces incorrect pricing and potential revenue loss. The system must enforce a single discount path to maintain price integrity.","testable_assertion":"When a customer applies a discount code to an order containing a product with an active promotion, the system must apply exactly one discount mechanism and reject or override the other."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"An order cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification would break financial reconciliation, payment processor records, and audit trails. This is a fundamental state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change line items, quantities, pricing, or shipping details on an order with status 'payment confirmed' or beyond must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling a product that has no inventory to fulfill is a physical constraint violation — you cannot ship what you do not have. Allowing it corrupts order fulfillment state.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to begin checkout (or complete a purchase) with a zero-inventory product must be rejected with an appropriate error, regardless of the product's catalog visibility."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 calendar days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business/customer-service policy. It could be extended to 60 days or shortened to 14 days by policy decision without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A refund request submitted on day 31 or later after delivery must be rejected. A request on day 30 must be accepted (assuming other criteria are met)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has added items to their cart","when":"The customer has not yet initiated checkout","then":"Available inventory counts for those items remain unchanged and no reservation records exist"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with items in their cart","when":"All items have sufficient available inventory","then":"Inventory reservations are created for each line item, decrementing available inventory by the requested quantities"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer initiated checkout 15 minutes ago","when":"Payment has not been confirmed","then":"All inventory reservations for that checkout session are released and available inventory is restored"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An administrator attempts to create a second promotion for the same product","then":"The system rejects the new promotion (or requires deactivation of the existing one first) and returns an error"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order contains a product with an active promotional price","when":"The customer applies a discount code","then":"The system applies only one discount mechanism and clearly communicates which one is applied and which is rejected"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order has payment confirmed","when":"Any actor attempts to modify the order contents, quantities, or pricing","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with zero available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to add it to cart and proceed to checkout","then":"The purchase is blocked with a clear out-of-stock message; the product may remain visible but is not purchasable"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order was delivered 30 days ago or fewer","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order was delivered more than 30 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message indicating the refund window has expired"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A customer initiates checkout","when":"One or more cart items have zero available inventory at the moment of reservation","then":"Checkout is blocked for the out-of-stock items and the customer is informed which items cannot be reserved"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Two customers checkout simultaneously for the last unit of a product","expected_behavior":"Exactly one checkout receives the reservation; the other is rejected with an out-of-stock error. Inventory must never go negative. This requires atomic reservation logic."},{"scenario":"Checkout timer expires at the exact moment payment is submitted","expected_behavior":"The system must resolve the race deterministically — either the payment is accepted (reservation still valid) or rejected (reservation already released). A payment should not succeed against released inventory."},{"scenario":"A promotion is deactivated mid-checkout after pricing was shown","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored for that session. Changing price mid-checkout violates customer expectations and may have legal implications."},{"scenario":"Customer applies a discount code, then a promotion is activated on the product before payment","expected_behavior":"The discount mechanism chosen at checkout time should be honored. Late-arriving promotions should not retroactively alter the checkout session's pricing."},{"scenario":"Product inventory drops to zero after cart-add but before checkout","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation must fail for the zero-inventory item. Cart contents are aspirational, not guaranteed."},{"scenario":"Refund requested exactly on the 30th day at 23:59:59","expected_behavior":"The request must be accepted. Boundary condition: 30 days means the entire 30th calendar day inclusive."},{"scenario":"Order delivered but delivery date is disputed by customer","expected_behavior":"Spec does not address dispute resolution. The refund window is anchored to the recorded delivery date. If the delivery date record is wrong, the refund window calculation is wrong — this is an operational risk."},{"scenario":"Discount code applied to an order where only some items have promotions","expected_behavior":"Spec says discount codes cannot combine with promotional pricing per order, not per item. Clarification needed: does one promotional item disqualify the entire order from a discount code, or only that line item?"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect inventory reservation could lead to overselling (fulfillment costs, customer compensation) or underselling (lost revenue from unnecessary stock-outs). Discount stacking bugs are a direct revenue leak. The 15-minute reservation window balances conversion against inventory lock-up cost.","operational":"Reservation timeout mechanism requires reliable scheduled job or TTL-based infrastructure. Race conditions on last-unit checkout demand transactional integrity. Promotion mutual exclusivity requires validation at both creation and checkout time.","user":"Customers see products they cannot buy (zero-inventory but listed) — this creates friction but is explicitly allowed by the spec. The single-discount-mechanism rule may frustrate deal-seeking customers who expect to stack coupons. Clear messaging is essential.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Race conditions on inventory reservation causing oversell, (2) Timer-based reservation release failing silently, leaving phantom reservations that block real sales, (3) Ambiguity in discount-vs-promotion precedence causing inconsistent pricing, (4) Refund window boundary disputes without delivery date verification.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Cannot stack discounts; may see listed products they cannot buy; orders become immutable after payment; refund window is strictly enforced."},{"group":"Product/Merchandising Team","impact":"Must manage one-promotion-per-product constraint; cannot run overlapping campaigns on the same SKU."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment simplifies reconciliation. Refund window provides a bounded liability period."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle edge cases around refund window boundaries, delivery date disputes, and customer frustration with discount exclusivity."},{"group":"Engineering/Platform","impact":"Must implement atomic inventory reservation, reliable timer-based release, and mutual exclusion logic for discounts — all with race-condition safety."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Depends on reservation accuracy. Overselling due to reservation bugs creates fulfillment exceptions and shipping delays."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively clear and internally consistent for most rules. However, key ambiguities exist: (1) the precedence rule when both a discount code and promotional pricing are present (which one wins?) is unspecified, (2) whether the discount/promotion mutual exclusion applies per-order or per-line-item is ambiguous, (3) the refund window boundary condition (inclusive vs exclusive of day 30) is not explicit, (4) no mention of what happens to reservations if the system crashes mid-checkout. These are resolvable ambiguities, not contradictions, so confidence remains above 55 but below 75.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is not reserved at cart-add time","15-minute reservation timeout policy","One active promotion per product","Order immutability after payment","Zero-inventory products cannot be purchased","30-day refund window existence"],"low_confidence_areas":["Discount code vs promotion precedence — which one takes priority when both apply?","Per-order vs per-line-item discount exclusivity","Refund window boundary precision (calendar days, timezone, inclusive/exclusive)","Checkout price-lock behavior when promotions change mid-session","Whether 'cannot be purchased' means blocked at cart-add or at checkout initiation"],"assumptions":["Delivery date is reliably recorded and undisputed","The 15-minute timer starts at checkout initiation, not at some other event","Refund window of 30 days means 30 calendar days inclusive of day 30","Zero-inventory check occurs at checkout initiation (reservation time), not at cart-add","Discount code vs promotion: only one applies per order, but the spec does not define which takes precedence — assumed the system rejects the second one applied"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When an order contains a product with an active promotion AND the customer applies a discount code, which discount mechanism takes precedence? The spec says they cannot be combined but does not specify which one wins or whether the customer chooses.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Merchandising Team","Finance/Accounting","Engineering/Platform"],"options":["A: Promotional pricing always wins (discount code is rejected)","B: Discount code always wins (promotional price is overridden)","C: Customer chooses which discount to apply","D: Whichever produces the greater discount for the customer applies automatically"],"recommendation":"Option C (customer chooses) provides the best UX and avoids surprising the customer, but Option A (promotion wins) is simplest to implement. Recommend clarifying with product owner."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Does the discount/promotion mutual exclusion apply at the order level or the line-item level? If an order has 3 items and only 1 has a promotion, can a discount code apply to the other 2 items?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting","Engineering/Platform"],"options":["A: Order-level exclusion — if any item has a promotion, no discount code can be used on the entire order","B: Line-item exclusion — discount code applies to non-promotional items, promotional pricing applies to promotional items"],"recommendation":"Option B (line-item level) is more customer-friendly and industry-standard, but the spec says 'per order' which suggests Option A. Needs product owner clarification."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"At what point is a zero-inventory product blocked from purchase? When added to cart, or when checkout is initiated? The spec says it 'cannot be purchased' but the cart is not a purchase.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Engineering/Platform"],"options":["A: Block at cart-add time — customer cannot add zero-inventory item to cart","B: Allow cart-add but block at checkout initiation — customer is informed when they try to buy"],"recommendation":"Option B aligns with the spec's separation of cart (no reservation) and checkout (reservation). Recommend blocking at checkout with clear messaging, but allowing cart-add for wishlist-like behavior."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
